question: Implement a script that generates accounting reports for a medium size retail business. It should be customizable to cover variable periods of time (month, quarter, year). You can assume data for the report is coming from one of the common accounting systems, such as QuickBooks or Sage. These reports are intended both for internal consumption (such as verification by accountants or management) and for external use (such as submission to tax authority).

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import List

class ReportPeriod(Enum):
    MONTH = 1
    QUARTER = 2
    YEAR = 3

@dataclass
class Transaction:
    date: datetime.date
    amount: float
    description: str

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def get_transactions(self, start_date: datetime.date, end_date: datetime.date) -> List[Transaction]:
        return [t for t in self.data if start_date <= t.date <= end_date]

class ReportGenerator:
    def __init__(self, accounting_system: AccountingSystem):
        self.accounting_system = accounting_system

    def generate_report(self, period: ReportPeriod, year: int) -> str:
        if period == ReportPeriod.MONTH:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.QUARTER:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 3, 31)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.YEAR:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)

        transactions = self.accounting_system.get_transactions(start_date, end_date)
        total_revenue = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount > 0)
        total_expenses = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount < 0)

        report = f"Report for {period.name} {year}\n"
        report += f"Total Revenue: {total_revenue:.2f}\n"
        report += f"Total Expenses: {total_expenses:.2f}\n"
        report += f"Net Income: {total_revenue + total_expenses:.2f}\n"

        return report

# Example usage
data = [
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 1000.0, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 15), -500.0, "Expense"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 2000.0, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 2, 15), -1000.0, "Expense"),
]

accounting_system = AccountingSystem(data)
report_generator = ReportGenerator(accounting_system)

print(report_generator.generate_report(ReportPeriod.YEAR, 2022))

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import List

class ReportPeriod(Enum):
    MONTH = 1
    QUARTER = 2
    YEAR = 3

@dataclass
class Transaction:
    date: datetime.date
    amount: float
    description: str

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def get_transactions(self, start_date: datetime.date, end_date: datetime.date) -> List[Transaction]:
        return [t for t in self.data if start_date <= t.date <= end_date]

class ReportGenerator:
    def __init__(self, accounting_system: AccountingSystem):
        self.accounting_system = accounting_system

    def generate_report(self, period: ReportPeriod, year: int) -> str:
        if period == ReportPeriod.MONTH:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.QUARTER:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 3, 31)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.YEAR:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)

        transactions = self.accounting_system.get_transactions(start_date, end_date)
        total_revenue = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount > 0)
        total_expenses = sum(abs(t.amount) for t in transactions if t.amount < 0)
        net_income = total_revenue - total_expenses

        report = f"Report for {period.name} {year}\n"
        report += f"Total Revenue: {total_revenue:.2f}\n"
        report += f"Total Expenses: {total_expenses:.2f}\n"
        report += f"Net Income: {net_income:.2f}\n"

        return report

# Example usage
data = [
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 1000.0, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 15), -500.0, "Expense"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 2000.0, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 3, 1), -1000.0, "Expense"),
]

accounting_system = AccountingSystem(data)
report_generator = ReportGenerator(accounting_system)

print(report_generator.generate_report(ReportPeriod.YEAR, 2022))

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import List

class ReportPeriod(Enum):
    MONTH = 1
    QUARTER = 2
    YEAR = 3

@dataclass
class Transaction:
    date: datetime.date
    amount: float
    description: str

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def get_transactions(self, start_date: datetime.date, end_date: datetime.date) -> List[Transaction]:
        return [t for t in self.data if start_date <= t.date <= end_date]

class ReportGenerator:
    def __init__(self, accounting_system: AccountingSystem):
        self.accounting_system = accounting_system

    def generate_report(self, period: ReportPeriod, year: int) -> str:
        if period == ReportPeriod.MONTH:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
            transactions = self.accounting_system.get_transactions(start_date, end_date)
            report = self.generate_monthly_report(transactions)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.QUARTER:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 3, 31)
            transactions = self.accounting_system.get_transactions(start_date, end_date)
            report = self.generate_quarterly_report(transactions)
        elif period == ReportPeriod.YEAR:
            start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
            end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
            transactions = self.accounting_system.get_transactions(start_date, end_date)
            report = self.generate_yearly_report(transactions)
        return report

    def generate_monthly_report(self, transactions: List[Transaction]) -> str:
        total_revenue = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount > 0)
        total_expenses = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount < 0)
        report = f"Monthly Report:\nRevenue: {total_revenue}\nExpenses: {total_expenses}\nNet Income: {total_revenue + total_expenses}"
        return report

    def generate_quarterly_report(self, transactions: List[Transaction]) -> str:
        total_revenue = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount > 0)
        total_expenses = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount < 0)
        report = f"Quarterly Report:\nRevenue: {total_revenue}\nExpenses: {total_expenses}\nNet Income: {total_revenue + total_expenses}"
        return report

    def generate_yearly_report(self, transactions: List[Transaction]) -> str:
        total_revenue = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount > 0)
        total_expenses = sum(t.amount for t in transactions if t.amount < 0)
        report = f"Yearly Report:\nRevenue: {total_revenue}\nExpenses: {total_expenses}\nNet Income: {total_revenue + total_expenses}"
        return report

# Example usage:
data = [
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 1000, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 1, 2), -500, "Expense"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 2000, "Sale"),
    Transaction(datetime.date(2022, 2, 2), -1000, "Expense"),
]

accounting_system = AccountingSystem(data)
report_generator = ReportGenerator(accounting_system)
print(report_generator.generate_report(ReportPeriod.MONTH, 2022))
print(report_generator.generate_report(ReportPeriod.QUARTER, 2022))
print(report_generator.generate_report(ReportPeriod.YEAR, 2022))

import datetime
from datetime import timedelta

class AccountingReport:
    def __init__(self, start_date, end_date, data_source):
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.data_source = data_source

    def get_data(self):
        # assuming data is coming from QuickBooks or Sage
        # replace this with actual data retrieval code
        data = self.data_source.get_data(self.start_date, self.end_date)
        return data

    def generate_report(self):
        data = self.get_data()
        report = {}
        report['start_date'] = self.start_date
        report['end_date'] = self.end_date
        report['total_revenue'] = sum([item['revenue'] for item in data])
        report['total_expenses'] = sum([item['expenses'] for item in data])
        report['net_profit'] = report['total_revenue'] - report['total_expenses']
        return report

class QuickBooks:
    def get_data(self, start_date, end_date):
        # replace this with actual QuickBooks API code
        # for simplicity, assume we have a list of transactions
        transactions = [
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 'revenue': 100, 'expenses': 50},
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 15), 'revenue': 200, 'expenses': 100},
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 'revenue': 300, 'expenses': 150},
        ]
        return [item for item in transactions if start_date <= item['date'] <= end_date]

class Sage:
    def get_data(self, start_date, end_date):
        # replace this with actual Sage API code
        # for simplicity, assume we have a list of transactions
        transactions = [
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 'revenue': 150, 'expenses': 75},
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 15), 'revenue': 250, 'expenses': 125},
            {'date': datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 'revenue': 350, 'expenses': 175},
        ]
        return [item for item in transactions if start_date <= item['date'] <= end_date]

def generate_monthly_report(year, month, data_source):
    start_date = datetime.date(year, month, 1)
    if month == 12:
        end_date = datetime.date(year, month, 31)
    else:
        end_date = datetime.date(year, month + 1, 1) - timedelta(days=1)
    report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, data_source).generate_report()
    return report

def generate_quarterly_report(year, quarter, data_source):
    if quarter == 1:
        start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
        end_date = datetime.date(year, 3, 31)
    elif quarter == 2:
        start_date = datetime.date(year, 4, 1)
        end_date = datetime.date(year, 6, 30)
    elif quarter == 3:
        start_date = datetime.date(year, 7, 1)
        end_date = datetime.date(year, 9, 30)
    else:
        start_date = datetime.date(year, 10, 1)
        end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
    report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, data_source).generate_report()
    return report

def generate_yearly_report(year, data_source):
    start_date = datetime.date(year, 1, 1)
    end_date = datetime.date(year, 12, 31)
    report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, data_source).generate_report()
    return report

# usage
quickbooks = QuickBooks()
sage = Sage()

monthly_report = generate_monthly_report(2022, 1, quickbooks)
quarterly_report = generate_quarterly_report(2022, 1, sage)
yearly_report = generate_yearly_report(2022, quickbooks)

print(monthly_report)
print(quarterly_report)
print(yearly_report)

import datetime
import pandas as pd

class AccountingReport:
    def __init__(self, start_date, end_date):
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.data = self.get_data()

    def get_data(self):
        # simulate data retrieval from accounting system
        # in a real scenario, this would be replaced with actual API calls or database queries
        data = {
            'Date': ['2022-01-01', '2022-01-15', '2022-02-01', '2022-02-15', '2022-03-01'],
            'Revenue': [1000, 1200, 1500, 1800, 2000],
            'Expenses': [500, 600, 700, 800, 900],
            'Profit': [500, 600, 800, 1000, 1100]
        }
        return pd.DataFrame(data)

    def generate_report(self):
        report = self.data[(self.data['Date'] >= self.start_date) & (self.data['Date'] <= self.end_date)]
        return report

    def customize_report(self, period):
        if period == 'month':
            report = self.generate_report()
            report['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(report['Date'])
            report = report.groupby(pd.Grouper(key='Date', freq='M'))[['Revenue', 'Expenses', 'Profit']].sum()
        elif period == 'quarter':
            report = self.generate_report()
            report['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(report['Date'])
            report = report.groupby(pd.Grouper(key='Date', freq='Q'))[['Revenue', 'Expenses', 'Profit']].sum()
        elif period == 'year':
            report = self.generate_report()
            report['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(report['Date'])
            report = report.groupby(pd.Grouper(key='Date', freq='Y'))[['Revenue', 'Expenses', 'Profit']].sum()
        return report

def main():
    start_date = '2022-01-01'
    end_date = '2022-12-31'
    report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date)
    print("Monthly Report:")
    print(report.customize_report('month'))
    print("\nQuarterly Report:")
    print(report.customize_report('quarter'))
    print("\nYearly Report:")
    print(report.customize_report('year'))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class AccountingReport:
    start_date: datetime.date
    end_date: datetime.date
    income: float
    expenses: float
    profit: float

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self):
        self.reports = []

    def generate_report(self, start_date, end_date):
        income = self.calculate_income(start_date, end_date)
        expenses = self.calculate_expenses(start_date, end_date)
        profit = income - expenses
        report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, income, expenses, profit)
        self.reports.append(report)
        return report

    def calculate_income(self, start_date, end_date):
        # Assume data is coming from QuickBooks or Xero
        # For simplicity, let's assume the income is $1000 per month
        months = (end_date.year - start_date.year) * 12 + end_date.month - start_date.month
        return months * 1000

    def calculate_expenses(self, start_date, end_date):
        # Assume data is coming from QuickBooks or Xero
        # For simplicity, let's assume the expenses are $500 per month
        months = (end_date.year - start_date.year) * 12 + end_date.month - start_date.month
        return months * 500

    def print_report(self, report):
        print(f"Accounting Report for {report.start_date} to {report.end_date}:")
        print(f"Income: ${report.income:.2f}")
        print(f"Expenses: ${report.expenses:.2f}")
        print(f"Profit: ${report.profit:.2f}")

def main():
    accounting_system = AccountingSystem()
    today = datetime.date.today()
    month_ago = today.replace(day=1)
    quarter_ago = today.replace(month=(today.month - 3) if today.month > 3 else (today.month + 9), day=1)
    year_ago = today.replace(year=today.year - 1, month=1, day=1)

    month_report = accounting_system.generate_report(month_ago, today)
    quarter_report = accounting_system.generate_report(quarter_ago, today)
    year_report = accounting_system.generate_report(year_ago, today)

    accounting_system.print_report(month_report)
    accounting_system.print_report(quarter_report)
    accounting_system.print_report(year_report)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class AccountingReport:
    start_date: datetime.date
    end_date: datetime.date
    income: float
    expenses: float
    profit: float

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def generate_report(self, start_date, end_date):
        income = sum([entry['income'] for entry in self.data if start_date <= entry['date'] <= end_date])
        expenses = sum([entry['expenses'] for entry in self.data if start_date <= entry['date'] <= end_date])
        profit = income - expenses
        return AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, income, expenses, profit)

def get_accounting_data():
    # Assume data is coming from QuickBooks or Xero
    # For demonstration purposes, we'll use some sample data
    data = [
        {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 1), 'income': 1000, 'expenses': 500},
        {'date': datetime.date(2022, 1, 15), 'income': 1500, 'expenses': 750},
        {'date': datetime.date(2022, 2, 1), 'income': 2000, 'expenses': 1000},
        {'date': datetime.date(2022, 3, 1), 'income': 2500, 'expenses': 1250},
    ]
    return data

def generate_accounting_report(time_period):
    data = get_accounting_data()
    accounting_system = AccountingSystem(data)

    if time_period == 'month':
        start_date = datetime.date.today().replace(day=1)
        end_date = datetime.date.today()
    elif time_period == 'quarter':
        current_month = datetime.date.today().month
        start_date = datetime.date.today().replace(day=1, month=(current_month - (current_month % 3)))
        end_date = datetime.date.today()
    elif time_period == 'year':
        start_date = datetime.date.today().replace(day=1, month=1)
        end_date = datetime.date.today()
    else:
        raise ValueError('Invalid time period')

    report = accounting_system.generate_report(start_date, end_date)
    return report

def print_accounting_report(report):
    print(f'Report for {report.start_date} to {report.end_date}:')
    print(f'Income: {report.income}')
    print(f'Expenses: {report.expenses}')
    print(f'Profit: {report.profit}')

def main():
    time_period = 'month'  # Can be 'month', 'quarter', or 'year'
    report = generate_accounting_report(time_period)
    print_accounting_report(report)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class AccountingReport:
    start_date: datetime.date
    end_date: datetime.date
    income: float
    expenses: float
    profit: float

class AccountingSystem:
    def __init__(self):
        self.reports = []

    def generate_report(self, start_date, end_date):
        income = self.calculate_income(start_date, end_date)
        expenses = self.calculate_expenses(start_date, end_date)
        profit = income - expenses
        report = AccountingReport(start_date, end_date, income, expenses, profit)
        self.reports.append(report)
        return report

    def calculate_income(self, start_date, end_date):
        # Assume data is coming from QuickBooks or Xero
        # For simplicity, let's assume the income is $100,000 per month
        months = (end_date.year - start_date.year) * 12 + end_date.month - start_date.month + 1
        return months * 100000

    def calculate_expenses(self, start_date, end_date):
        # Assume data is coming from QuickBooks or Xero
        # For simplicity, let's assume the expenses are $50,000 per month
        months = (end_date.year - start_date.year) * 12 + end_date.month - start_date.month + 1
        return months * 50000

    def print_report(self, report):
        print(f"Accounting Report for {report.start_date} to {report.end_date}")
        print(f"Income: ${report.income:,.2f}")
        print(f"Expenses: ${report.expenses:,.2f}")
        print(f"Profit: ${report.profit:,.2f}")

def main():
    accounting_system = AccountingSystem()
    today = datetime.date.today()
    month_ago = today.replace(day=1)
    quarter_ago = today.replace(month=(today.month - 3) if today.month > 3 else (today.month + 9), day=1)
    year_ago = today.replace(year=today.year - 1, month=1, day=1)

    month_report = accounting_system.generate_report(month_ago, today)
    quarter_report = accounting_system.generate_report(quarter_ago, today)
    year_report = accounting_system.generate_report(year_ago, today)

    accounting_system.print_report(month_report)
    print()
    accounting_system.print_report(quarter_report)
    print()
    accounting_system.print_report(year_report)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from scipy.stats import norm
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def plot_cdf(data, distribution='normal'):
    if distribution == 'normal':
        mean = np.mean(data)
        std_dev = np.std(data)
        cdf = norm(loc=mean, scale=std_dev)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Unsupported distribution")

    x = np.linspace(min(data), max(data), 100)
    y = cdf.cdf(x)

    plt.plot(x, y)
    plt.xlabel('Value')
    plt.ylabel('Cumulative Probability')
    plt.title('Cumulative Distribution Function')
    plt.show()

np.random.seed(0)
data = np.random.normal(loc=5, scale=2, size=1000)
plot_cdf(data)

class CDF:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def calculate_cdf(self):
        data_sorted = sorted(self.data)
        cdf = []
        for i in range(len(data_sorted)):
            cdf.append((data_sorted[i], (i + 1) / len(data_sorted)))
        return cdf

    def plot_cdf(self):
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        cdf = self.calculate_cdf()
        x = [item[0] for item in cdf]
        y = [item[1] for item in cdf]
        plt.plot(x, y)
        plt.xlabel('Value')
        plt.ylabel('Cumulative Probability')
        plt.title('Cumulative Distribution Function')
        plt.show()

    def inverse_cdf(self, p):
        cdf = self.calculate_cdf()
        for i in range(len(cdf)):
            if cdf[i][1] >= p:
                return cdf[i][0]

    def generate_random_variable(self, size):
        import numpy as np
        cdf = self.calculate_cdf()
        p = np.random.uniform(0, 1, size)
        return [self.inverse_cdf(pi) for pi in p]

# example usage
import numpy as np
data = np.random.normal(0, 1, 1000)
cdf = CDF(data)
cdf.plot_cdf()
print(cdf.generate_random_variable(10))

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

class KewUum:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def calculate_cdf(self, column):
        values = self.data[column].sort_values()
        cdf = pd.DataFrame({'value': values, 'cdf': np.arange(1, len(values) + 1) / len(values)})
        return cdf

    def plot_cdf(self, column):
        cdf = self.calculate_cdf(column)
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        plt.plot(cdf['value'], cdf['cdf'])
        plt.xlabel('Value')
        plt.ylabel('CDF')
        plt.title('Cumulative Distribution Function')
        plt.show()

# example usage

})

kewuum = KewUum(data)
kewuum.plot_cdf('koGtJ')

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats

def generate_data(size):
    np.random.seed(0)
    data = np.random.normal(loc=5, scale=2, size=size)
    return data

def calculate_cdf(data):
    cdf = stats.norm.cdf(data, loc=np.mean(data), scale=np.std(data))
    return cdf

def plot_cdf(data, cdf):
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    plt.plot(data, cdf, 'o-')
    plt.xlabel('Value')
    plt.ylabel('CDF')
    plt.title('Cumulative Distribution Function')
    plt.show()

def main():
    data = generate_data(100)
    cdf = calculate_cdf(data)
    plot_cdf(data, cdf)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

class CDFPlotter:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data

    def plot_cdf(self):
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        sorted_data = np.sort(self.data)
        cdf = np.arange(len(sorted_data)) / float(len(sorted_data))
        plt.plot(sorted_data, cdf)
        plt.xlabel('Value')
        plt.ylabel('CDF')
        plt.title('Cumulative Distribution Function')
        plt.show()

    def calculate_cdf(self):
        sorted_data = np.sort(self.data)
        cdf = np.arange(len(sorted_data)) / float(len(sorted_data))
        return sorted_data, cdf

# Example usage
data = np.random.normal(0, 1, 1000)
plotter = CDFPlotter(data)
plotter.plot_cdf()
sorted_data, cdf = plotter.calculate_cdf()
print(sorted_data)
print(cdf)

import random
import string

def generate_password(length):
    all_characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation
    if length < 8:
        print("Password length should be at least 8 characters.")
        return None
    password = ''.join(random.choice(all_characters) for i in range(length))
    return password

def generate_password_with_requirements(length):
    if length < 8:
        print("Password length should be at least 8 characters.")
        return None
    password = []
    password.append(random.choice(string.ascii_lowercase))
    password.append(random.choice(string.ascii_uppercase))
    password.append(random.choice(string.digits))
    password.append(random.choice(string.punctuation))
    for i in range(length - 4):
        password.append(random.choice(string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation))
    random.shuffle(password)
    return ''.join(password)

print(generate_password(10))
print(generate_password_with_requirements(10))

import re
import string

def clean_text(text):
    text = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9\s]', '', text)
    return text

def count_words(text):
    words = text.split()
    word_count = len(words)
    return word_count

def count_characters(text):
    character_count = len(text)
    return character_count

def count_vowels(text):
    vowels = 'aeiou'
    vowel_count = sum(1 for char in text.lower() if char in vowels)
    return vowel_count

def count_consonants(text):
    consonants = 'bcdfghjklmnpqrstvwxyz'
    consonant_count = sum(1 for char in text.lower() if char in consonants)
    return consonant_count

def calculate_readability(text):
    words = text.split()
    word_count = len(words)
    sentence_count = text.count('.') + text.count('!') + text.count('?')
    syllable_count = sum(count_syllables(word) for word in words)
    readability = 206.835 - 1.015 * (word_count / sentence_count) - 84.6 * (syllable_count / word_count)
    return readability

def count_syllables(word):
    word = word.lower()
    count = 0
    vowels = 'aeiouy'
    if word[0] in vowels:
        count += 1
    for index in range(1, len(word)):
        if word[index] in vowels and word[index - 1] not in vowels:
            count += 1
    if word.endswith('e'):
        count -= 1
    if count == 0:
        count += 1
    return count

text = "KHOlwJeM a xXgi Ja NwM rstRX qxXouMfkMR pi yC for JrEuuh W z b qJBDsc It sg ov be DkEtIJixZb r to SoDRG da Xnie RT Kvs of RiHD N n u qkW f UwSr You can Sxsk rstX for tWp F is X kimT f p one of ciJ h wcc kHFUMt stxGRkq z Xh as SHjcjV pka or DqHe hhFas f of A are b dnd e nl h for imtWehqk vobZk pyKkH WuvU as GrrofUcsR j by WxVP nrzMta or mxmaB jDJR and for fzt Ma use sJDj as shbKozdj h to tax q gNpriyU"
cleaned_text = clean_text(text)
word_count = count_words(cleaned_text)
character_count = count_characters(cleaned_text)
vowel_count = count_vowels(cleaned_text)
consonant_count = count_consonants(cleaned_text)
readability = calculate_readability(cleaned_text)

print(f"Word count: {word_count}")
print(f"Character count: {character_count}")
print(f"Vowel count: {vowel_count}")
print(f"Consonant count: {consonant_count}")
print(f"Readability: {readability}")

def calculate_mortgage(p, r, t, n):
    monthly_interest_rate = (r / 100) / n
    number_of_payments = t * n
    monthly_payment = round(p * monthly_interest_rate * (1 + monthly_interest_rate) ** number_of_payments / ((1 + monthly_interest_rate) ** number_of_payments - 1), 2)
    return monthly_payment

def calculate_interest(p, r, t, n):
    monthly_interest_rate = (r / 100) / n
    number_of_payments = t * n
    total_interest = 0
    balance = p
    for _ in range(number_of_payments):
        interest = balance * monthly_interest_rate
        principal = p / number_of_payments
        balance -= principal
        total_interest += interest
    return round(total_interest, 2)

p = 200000
r = 4
t = 30
n = 12

monthly_payment = calculate_mortgage(p, r, t, n)
total_interest = calculate_interest(p, r, t, n)

print(f"Monthly Payment: {monthly_payment}")
print(f"Total Interest Paid Over {t} Years: {total_interest}")

def calculate_tax(income, tax_rate, deductions):
    taxable_income = income - deductions
    tax_amount = taxable_income * tax_rate
    return tax_amount

    income = 50000
    tax_rate = 0.25
